skill.name: tdd-workflow
skill.description: Guides Test-Driven Development workflows for Node.js and TypeScript projects using examples for tests, fixtures, doubles, and refactoring.
skill.summary: Apply RED-GREEN-REFACTOR practices to TypeScript projects with focused test patterns.
skill.author: DoubleslashSE

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak crypto, SQLite, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only Markdown documentation and TypeScript test examples. No executable scripts, network calls, prompt injection text, credential handling, or malicious behavior were found in SKILL.md or patterns.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Backticks
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Markdown code fences and inline examples as Ruby shell backtick execution. The reviewed locations are TypeScript documentation examples, not executable Ruby or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced TypeScript examples inside Markdown files. No shell interpreter, Ruby code, or runtime hook is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings do not correspond to cryptographic operations. The cited lines contain TDD descriptions and TypeScript fixture examples, with no hashing or encryption calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited files found no MD5, SHA-1, cipher, or password storage implementation. The scanner appears to have matched benign text or Markdown context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Generic Database and Test Data Examples
low_findings.description: The SQLite and reconnaissance findings map to generic fake database, email, and logger examples used for testing guidance. No real database file, system probe, or host inspection behavior exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is confined to documentation examples for tests and dependency injection. I found no file access, command execution, OS enumeration, or real SQLite artifact.

content.value_statement: Teams often write tests after implementation, which can hide unclear behavior and fragile designs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through RED-GREEN-REFACTOR workflows for Node.js and TypeScript.
actual_capabilities: Explains the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle for TypeScript work.
Provides Arrange-Act-Assert examples for unit tests.
Shows naming conventions for behavior-focused test cases.
Describes unit, integration, and contract test categories.
Documents stubs, mocks, fakes, and spies for test doubles.
Gives patterns for builders, factories, fixtures, and fake repositories.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not run tests by itself.
Examples focus on Node.js, TypeScript, and Jest-style syntax.
It does not include project-specific framework setup steps.
It does not replace security or performance testing guidance.

use_cases.title: Start a Tested Feature
use_cases.description: Plan failing tests before implementation and keep each change tied to observable behavior.

use_cases.title: Improve Test Structure
use_cases.description: Standardize test names, AAA layout, fixtures, and test doubles across a TypeScript codebase.

use_cases.title: Review Test Quality
use_cases.description: Identify brittle assertions, implementation-detail checks, and interdependent tests during review.

prompt_templates.title: Create First TDD Test
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a small feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tdd-workflow skill. Help me write the first failing test for this TypeScript behavior: [describe behavior]. Keep the test focused on observable output.

prompt_templates.title: Plan RED-GREEN-REFACTOR Steps
prompt_templates.scenario: Break a feature into safe implementation increments.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tdd-workflow skill. Create a RED-GREEN-REFACTOR plan for [feature]. Include test cases, minimal implementation steps, and refactor checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Design Test Doubles
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose mocks, stubs, fakes, or spies for dependencies.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tdd-workflow skill. Review these dependencies: [list dependencies]. Recommend suitable test doubles and explain what each test should verify.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tdd-workflow skill. Audit these tests for brittle assertions, implementation details, shared state, and missing behavior coverage: [paste test summary].

output_examples.input: Plan tests for an email validation function.
output: A list of valid and invalid email scenarios.
A suggested behavior-focused test name for each scenario.
A minimal GREEN implementation plan after the first failing test.

output_examples.input: Review tests for a user service.
output: Findings about implementation-detail assertions and shared state.
Recommendations for fakes, mocks, and fixtures.
A refactor order that keeps tests passing after each step.

output_examples.input: Create a test strategy for an order workflow.
output: Unit tests for pure decision logic.
Integration tests for repository and payment boundaries.
Contract checks for returned order status and error shapes.

best_practices: Write the failing test before changing production code.
Keep each test focused on one observable behavior.
Refactor only after the relevant tests are green.
anti_patterns: Testing private state instead of public behavior.
Using overly specific assertions that break on harmless changes.
Letting tests depend on execution order or shared mutable state.

faq.question: Does this skill execute test commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides TDD guidance and examples. The user or agent must run project commands separately.

faq.question: Which languages does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Node.js and TypeScript projects, with examples that resemble Jest test syntax.

faq.question: Can it help with integration tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains integration test boundaries and shows dependency injection patterns for controlled tests.

faq.question: Does it include test data patterns?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers builders, factory functions, object mothers, and reusable test contexts.

faq.question: Is this useful for code review?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists common test anti-patterns and helps reviewers focus on behavior, isolation, and maintainability.

faq.question: Does it teach security testing?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on general TDD workflow and test design, not specialized security testing.
